answer: The main sound changes from Old Chinese to Middle Chinese include: (1) Loss of final -j in -aj sequences, which become -é or -á; (2) Loss of glottal stops marked by ?; (3) Simplification of initial consonant clusters; (4) Vowel shifts including ə→ö/ä/a; (4) Some initial consonants undergo palatalization; (5) The apostrophe marking (pharyngealization) is lost; (6) Final stops are sometimes preserved but often modified or lost.
